title: signals: Define SignalData struct and key encoding per ADR-007

resolved_clarifications.question: What encoding separates invocation_id from signal_name in the key?
What is the tree name for signals?
resolved_clarifications.answer: SIGNALS = b'signals' per ADR-007 tree definitions

assumptions.assumption: sled::Db is available from wtf-storage crate
serde is used for JSON serialization
assumptions.validation_method: Check existing ADR-007 examples use serde

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL encode signal keys as 'invocation_id:signal_name'
THE SYSTEM SHALL serialize SignalData as JSON

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL insert SignalData into SIGNALS tree with composite key
THE SYSTEM SHALL return SignalData if found, None otherwise

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow ambiguous key encoding
unwanted.because: Colon is the key separator - would cause key collision

required_inputs.constraints: Non-empty, no ':' characters, ULID format recommended

system_state: SIGNALS tree must be accessible via db.open_tree(SIGNALS)

state_changes: New key-value pair inserted into SIGNALS tree

return_guarantees.guarantee: Returns Ok(()) on success, Err on sled error
Returns Some(SignalData) if key exists, None if not found

side_effects: store_signal flushes to disk via flush_async

invariants: Key format always 'invocation_id:signal_name' with single colon separator
SignalData.received_at is always Unix timestamp in milliseconds

files_to_read.what_to_extract: SIGNALS tree definition, key encoding, SignalData structure

patterns_to_find.purpose: Understand how other trees are opened in this crate

data_integrity_failures.failure: Key collision if invocation_id or signal_name contains ':'
data_integrity_failures.prevention: Validate no ':' in inputs before key construction

happy_paths.name: test_signal_data_serialization_roundtrip
happy_paths.given: Valid SignalData with invocation_id='test123', name='signal1', payload=[1,2,3]

error_paths.name: test_store_signal_rejects_colon_in_invocation_id
then: Returns Err with message about invalid characters

tasks.task: Read ADR-007 and extract SignalData specification
tasks.done_when: SignalData fields and key encoding documented

tasks.what: Test SignalData JSON roundtrip and received_at timestamp

tasks.task: Implement SignalData struct with serde derives
tasks.what: Define SignalData { invocation_id, name, payload, received_at } with Serialize, Deserialize

tasks.depends_on: Implement SignalData struct with serde derives
tasks.what: Add make_signal_key(invocation_id, signal_name) -> Vec<u8> and validate_no_colon() helpers

related_files.relevance: Source of truth for SignalData schema and key encoding
Where signals module will be registered

similar_implementations: Timer storage in ADR-007 uses similar pattern: invocation_id:seq -> TimerData

do: Use Result<_, SignalError> for fallible operations
Follow existing tree patterns from ADR-007 examples
do_not: Do NOT use unwrap() on any fallible operation
Do NOT invent APIs not in sled or serde

title: signals: Implement store_signal and get_signal functions

resolved_clarifications.question: What error type to return?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Custom SignalError enum with StoreFailed, GetFailed, InvalidKey variants

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return Result from all fallible operations
THE SYSTEM SHALL use SignalError for all signal-related errors

event_driven.trigger: WHEN store_signal is called with valid inputs
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL insert SignalData into SIGNALS tree
THE SYSTEM SHALL flush the tree asynchronously
THE SYSTEM SHALL return Some(SignalData) if key exists

unwanted.condition: IF db is closed when store_signal is called
unwanted.because: Must return Result for proper error handling

state_changes: New entry exists in SIGNALS tree with key 'invocation_id:signal_name'

return_guarantees.guarantee: Ok(()) on success, Err(SignalError::StoreFailed) on sled error
Some(SignalData) if found, None if not found

side_effects: store_signal calls flush_async on the tree

invariants: SignalData.received_at is always current Unix timestamp in millis at time of store

prior_art.what_to_learn: Pattern for inserting JSON into sled tree with error handling

happy_paths.name: test_store_and_get_signal_roundtrip
then: get_signal returns Some with matching invocation_id, name, payload

tasks.what: Test store_signal followed by get_signal returns matching data
Test get_signal with non-existent key returns None
Define SignalError with variants: StoreFailed(String), GetFailed(String), InvalidKey(String)
pub fn store_signal(db: &sled::Db, invocation_id: &str, signal_name: &str, payload: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), SignalError>
pub fn get_signal(db: &sled::Db, invocation_id: &str, signal_name: &str) -> Result<Option<SignalData>, SignalError>

related_files.relevance: Pattern for journal insert shows same pattern needed here

similar_implementations: append_journal in ADR-007 uses same insert + flush_async pattern

do: Use db.open_tree(b"signals")? to get the SIGNALS tree
Use format!("{}:{}", invocation_id, signal_name) for key construction
do_not: Do NOT use unwrap() on SystemTime - use expect() with message or handle error properly

title: signals: Implement get_pending_signals with prefix scan

resolved_clarifications.question: How to scan all signals for an invocation_id?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Use scan_prefix with 'invocation_id:' prefix, parse and filter keys

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return all signals matching invocation_id prefix

return_guarantees.guarantee: Vec<SignalData> containing all signals for this invocation_id

prior_art.what_to_learn: Pattern for prefix scan: scan_prefix, parse key, collect entries

edge_cases.name: test_get_pending_signals_empty_for_unknown_invocation
edge_cases.scenario: No signals exist for given invocation_id

tasks.what: Store 3 signals, call get_pending_signals, verify all 3 returned
Call get_pending_signals with non-existent invocation_id, verify empty Vec
pub fn get_pending_signals(db: &sled::Db, invocation_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<SignalData>, SignalError>

related_files.relevance: get_journal shows scan_prefix pattern at lines 159-175

do: Use format!("{}:", invocation_id) as the prefix to scan
Collect into Vec and return Ok(vec)

title: signals: Implement delete_signal with cleanup semantics

resolved_clarifications.question: Does delete_signal need to return the deleted data?
resolved_clarifications.answer: No, just return Result<(), SignalError>. Caller already has the data if needed.

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL remove the signal from the SIGNALS tree

unwanted.condition: IF signal does not exist when delete is called
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return an error

state_changes: Signal key no longer exists in SIGNALS tree

return_guarantees.guarantee: Ok(()) whether or not signal existed

tasks.what: Store signal, delete it, verify get returns None
Call delete on non-existent signal, verify Ok(())
pub fn delete_signal(db: &sled::Db, invocation_id: &str, signal_name: &str) -> Result<(), SignalError>

do: Use tree.remove(key)? and ignore the returned Option
Return Ok(()) unconditionally after successful remove
do_not: Do NOT check if key exists before deleting

title: signals: Implement wait_for_signal with watch_prefix for async waiting

resolved_clarifications.question: What async runtime to use?
resolved_clarifications.answer: sled::Event via watch_channel - returns a Receiver that fires when key is set

resolved_clarifications.question: What is DurableFuture return type?
resolved_clarifications.answer: impl Future<Output = SignalData> - future that resolves when signal arrives

assumptions.assumption: tokio is the async runtime used by wtf-engine

event_driven.trigger: WHEN wait_for_signal is called and signal exists
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return immediately with the existing signal

event_driven.trigger: WHEN wait_for_signal is called and signal does not exist
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL wait until signal is stored, then return it

prior_art.what_to_learn: How watch_channel works for cross-thread notifications

external_docs.extract: How to use watch_channel to wait for key changes

usability_failures.failure: wait_for_signal hangs forever if signal never arrives
usability_failures.prevention: Caller should use timeout at call site

happy_paths.name: test_wait_returns_existing_signal_immediately
then: Returns immediately with the signal data

tasks.what: Store signal, call wait_for_signal, verify immediate return
pub async fn wait_for_signal(db: &sled::Db, invocation_id: &str, signal_name: &str) -> Result<SignalData, SignalError>
patterns_to_use: if let Some(existing) = get_signal()? { return Ok(existing); }

do: First check if signal already exists with get_signal
If not, call watch_prefix and await events
Filter events for the specific key we're waiting on
Deserialize and return SignalData when event received
do_not: Do NOT block indefinitely without check for existing signal first
